prompt: A haunting, cinematic visual metaphor showing the deadly impact of air pollution: a massive pair of human lungs formed from decaying industrial cities, with smokestacks emitting thick black smoke into a dark sky. The lungs are collapsing and deteriorating, with cracks and fire inside. From the center of the lungs, a cascade of human skulls pours downward like a waterfall, symbolizing deaths caused by pollution. At the bottom, a foggy graveyard filled with tombstones and silhouettes of people, including a child holding an adult’s hand, looking up in despair. Hyper-detailed, dramatic lighting, muted color palette, editorial illustration style, no text, no labels
dataset_name: Global Burden of Disease Study – Air Pollution
insight: Air pollution causes millions of deaths each year, silently turning the air we breathe into a deadly force.
